rules: RenderMan product names, renderer names, node names, API identifiers, file paths, environment variables, and UI labels with exact spelling must be preserved unless the glossary gives a Chinese rendering.
First occurrence of an important English technical acronym may use Chinese explanation plus acronym, e.g. '任意输出变量（AOV）'; subsequent occurrences may use the acronym.
Do not translate identifiers beginning with Pxr, Rix, Ri:, RfM, RfH, RfK, RfB, PRMan, RMAN, RMANTREE, or node/operator names from DCC applications.
Translate explanatory prose naturally, but keep command-line examples, code blocks, attribute names, parameter names, JSON keys, and file extensions unchanged.
Prefer established CG/VFX Chinese terminology: 渲染, 着色, 材质, 纹理, 采样, 积分器, 光路, 合成, 置换, 体积, 次表面散射, 法线, 反照率, 噪声, 降噪.

protected_terms.note: Common CG/VFX shorthand; can explain as 外观开发.
Hybrid CPU/GPU RenderMan renderer; explain as CPU/GPU 混合渲染器 when needed.
RenderMan Integrator System; usually keep acronym.
Digital Content Creation; may explain as 数字内容创作软件.
Universal Scene Description; first occurrence may be 通用场景描述（USD）.
Open Shading Language; first occurrence may be 开放着色语言（OSL）.
Arbitrary Output Variable; first occurrence may be 任意输出变量（AOV）.
Light Path Expression; first occurrence may be 光路表达式（LPE）.

items.text: Minimum System Requirements
The following operating systems are supported for this release. RenderMan may operate outside of this requirement, but it may be unstable and is not supported:
Compatible with any Linux distribution supporting gcc 11.2 and glibc 2.34 or higher (e.g. RHEL9.x and newer)
RenderMan is designed to efficiently scale across many CPU cores, so while the above is a minimum requirement, systems with multiple processors will see the best performance.
Pixar's RenderMan software is delivered as:
RenderMan-Installer-
version
-linux
flavor
.rpm - The RenderMan rendering toolkit.
The
flavor
string includes the common distribution, e.g. RHEL9, the
gcc/icc
versions, and the microprocessor instruction set. Putting them all together provides, for example, an RHEL package named RenderMan-Installer-27.0.0_2376203
-linuxRHEL9_gcc11icx232.x86_64.rpm
Note that users must set their environment variables manually on Linux to allow for using an executable from a terminal window. This process is explained below Command Line Installation.
You must also meet the requirements listed on the top-level
Installation and Licensing
page.
GUI-based Installation
Although RPM packages are typically installed via the command line, some Linux distributions have GUI-based package managers, like
gnomerpm

. Some distributions will also allow for double-clicking the RPM file in a directory browser, which will invoke RPM with default arguments. You can try this with your Linux-based system. Should it not begin the installation on its own (because you do not have the necessary components to double click an .rpm) then you may begin the process using the steps below.

The process below will apply to the 27.0 release, not the Beta. The 27.0 Beta does not include the GUI Installer. Instead, you can download each RPM and install it with a command like this (only the one for RenderManProServer is shown, but the other components can be installed similarly). Prefix the command with
if your system’s permissions require it:
To install RenderMan using the GUI Installer (27.0 release only), run:
Due to user security changes in EL9, it may be necessary to run the following command line before running the RenderMan Installer application if it fails to start under sudo:
The RenderMan RtxSVG.so plugin requires the compat-expat1 package be installed.  If you plan to use SVG textures, please install compat-expat1 first.
If you have already unpacked the .rpm, you can then run the following command to begin the GUI-based installer:

The installer will connect with the RenderMan Pixar Server and download your licensed components. During the installation process you will see that the default location for installation is /opt/pixar This will become the root directory for all of the software components listed above.
We'll now go step by step through the process.
First, you must log in with your forum account and provide the serial number for the license you have purchased.
Next, you may see the following if you are installing on a floating license server for the
FIRST
time:
This is telling you that the installer is about to key your license server to the host you are running the installer on.
If this is
NOT
the machine you want to run your license server on, press the
Cancel
button and re-run the installer on the machine you want to designate as the license server.

If you decide to make this machine your license server or you are installing a node-locked license, the installer will then contact Pixar and retrieve your license file. It will place this license file by default in:
Windows: C:\Program Files\Pixar\pixar.license
macOS:  /Applications/Pixar/pixar.license
Linux: /opt/pixar/pixar.license
Next, you will see a list of packages you wish to install.

The installer will try to find the appropriate plugins to install as well. However, if it cannot find a Maya installation or determines you have already installed the latest version of the software, it will give you a warning that something strange is afoot.
Here you can now decide which software and packages you wish to install. For instance, if you do not wish to install the Katana plugins, you can deselect those. If you
WOULD
like to install the license server for a floating license installation, you may select the "Show All" button and select the license server package as well.
Installing The License Server
talks in detail about the steps that must be taken on each platform once the installation finishes ensuring that the floating license server is functioning correctly.
Once the desired packages are selected, hitting the
Next
button goes to the download and installation phase.
Once this phase is complete a
Success!
screen should appear. At this point, you can exit the installer.
When the installer is finished, the floating license service has
NOT BEEN INSTALLED
or
STARTED.
Linux License Server
goes over all the details for installing and starting the floating license services.
Downloads

Other types of files besides RenderMan, the License Server, and the DCC plugins can be downloaded with the installer as well. These include the RenderMan Examples. These will be placed in the user who ran the RenderManInstaller Download directory.
Python
If you plan to take advantage of the Python binding you will need to download Python installer from
www.python.org
. RenderMan Pro Server 27 binding is compatible with 3.7,
Help
If you require further assistance, please check out the
RenderMan Support Forum
. Note that registration is required.
